text:
User: A session-scoped Stop hook is now active with condition: "fan out sonnet/haiku/opus agents to land ALL of this -- including verifying that it works as expected -- I don't want the split-brain thing -- I want to use trellis for real -- once everything is done and ready it should be using the tenex-edge-dev skill to validate with real containers that things work as expected and that we can validate things using Trellis

I expect you to provide frequent updates of this -- use the #trellis-tenex-edge channel (you need to create it) for this updates -- and, whenever something interesting has been accomplished use `say` to tell me about it (TTS) -- I would expect you would use `say` at least between 10 to 20 times throughout the execution of this whole task". Briefly acknowledge the goal, then immediately start (or continue) working toward it — treat the condition itself as your directive and do not pause to ask the user what to do. The hook will block stopping until the condition holds. It auto-clears once the condition is met — do not tell the user to run `/goal clear` after success; that's only for clearing a goal early. [...] Assistant: I branched off a codex feature branch with uncommitted changes in a repo where other agents are actively working — that's collision-prone for a large refactor. Let me set up an isolated worktree off `master` instead, so this big effort stays clean and doesn't stomp the live tree.

Assistant: Worktree ready at `~/src/tenex-edge-trellis` on `trellis-adoption` (off `origin/master`, HEAD #218). Now let me create the channel, post the kickoff, and kick off a baseline build to confirm a green starting point. [...] Assistant: The structure matches the earlier reports (code advanced to #218). Note the repo enforces a 500-LOC-per-file ceiling — new code must go in new modules. Let me check the baseline build result and read the two load-bearing files for the first surface.
